title: Eugenia bataanensis
intro: Eugenia bataanensis, also known as Bataan guava or guavita, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory diseases. Eugenia bataanensis is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eugenia bataanensis include tea infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Eugenia bataanensis is classified in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the myrtle family. This classification places it within the order Myrtales, which includes a large number of flowering plant species. Eugenia bataanensis is further classified as a species within the genus Eugenia, a genus that comprises over 1,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Eugenia is itself a part of the tribe Myrteae, which includes several other genera of flowering plants. The classification of Eugenia bataanensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Myrtaceae family.
